ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' / 'Contagious Interview' — ISO/LNK lure → script-engine → LOLBin downloader chain
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Lazarus Group (Diamond Sleet / Labyrinth Chollima / ZINC) recruitment-lure delivery chain: a user mounts an ISO/IMG/VHD or opens a LNK from a fake job-offer archive, explorer.exe spawns wscript/mshta/cmd/PowerShell, and within ~5min that stage-one process spawns a LOLBin (curl/certutil/bitsadmin/powershell) reaching out to a remote payload. This three-link chain has been the durable Dream Job / Contagious Interview pattern across Mandiant, Microsoft (Diamond Sleet), and ESET reporting on aerospace/defense and cryptocurrency targets since 2020.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.parent_process IN ("*.lnk*","*.iso*","*.img*","*.vhd*","*.zip*","*.pdf*") OR Processes.process IN ("*.lnk*","*.iso*","*.img*")) by _time host Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.process Processes.process_name span=1s | rename Processes.* AS * | rename _time AS lure_time, process_id AS lure_pid, process AS lure_cmd, process_name AS lure_proc | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe") AND (Processes.process="*http*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*-urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*-decode*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*") by _time host Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process Processes.process_name span=1s | rename Processes.* AS * | rename _time AS dl_time, parent_process_id AS lure_pid, process AS dl_cmd, process_name AS dl_proc ] | eval delay = dl_time - lure_time | where delay >= 0 AND delay <= 300 | table lure_time, host, user, lure_proc, lure_cmd, dl_time, dl_proc, dl_cmd, delay | sort - lure_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrWindowSec = 300;
let ScriptEngines = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let Downloaders   = dynamic(["curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let LureLaunch = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
    | where FileName in~ (ScriptEngines)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk",".iso",".img",".vhd",".pdf",".zip")
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk",".iso",".img")
    | project LureTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LurePid = ProcessId, LureProc = FileName, LureCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ (Downloaders)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","iwr ","-urlcache","-decode","IEX","FromBase64String")
| join kind=inner LureLaunch on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.LurePid
| where Timestamp between (LureTime .. LureTime + CorrWindowSec * 1s)
| project LureTime, ChainTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LureTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          LureProc, LureCmd,
          Downloader = FileName, DownloaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by ChainTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let CorrWindowSec = 300;
let ScriptEngines = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let Downloaders   = dynamic(["curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let LureLaunch = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend NewProc = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ParentProc = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentProc == "explorer.exe"
    | where NewProc in (ScriptEngines)
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (".lnk",".iso",".img",".vhd",".pdf",".zip")
         or CommandLine has_any (".lnk",".iso",".img")
    | project LureTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, LurePid = NewProcessId,
              LureProc = NewProc, LureCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend NewProc = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where NewProc in (Downloaders)
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","-urlcache","-decode","IEX","FromBase64String")
| join kind=inner LureLaunch on $left.Computer == $right.Computer, $left.ProcessId == $right.LurePid
| where TimeGenerated between (LureTime .. LureTime + CorrWindowSec * 1s)
| project LureTime, ChainTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LureTime),
          Computer, Account, LureProc, LureCmd,
          Downloader = NewProc, DownloaderCmd = CommandLine
| order by ChainTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus DreamJob/Contagious-Interview Lure - ISO or LNK Spawning Script Engine via Explorer
id: e6f3c1a0-1c7e-4e2a-9a1f-7c8d8b3a9e10
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects script engines spawned by explorer.exe with an ISO/IMG/VHD/LNK/PDF token in the parent command line.
    This is the durable first-stage shape of Lazarus Group (G0032 / Diamond Sleet / Labyrinth Chollima)
    'Operation Dream Job' and 'Contagious Interview' recruitment-themed lures targeting aerospace, defense,
    and cryptocurrency talent.
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
    - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/10/18/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-teamcity-cve-2023-42793-vulnerability/
    - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/north-korean-spyware-zinc
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.t1566.001
    - attack.execution
    - attack.t1204.002
    - attack.g0032
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
    selection_child:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\mshta.exe'
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\pwsh.exe'
    selection_lure:
        ParentCommandLine|contains:
            - '.lnk'
            - '.iso'
            - '.img'
            - '.vhd'
            - '.pdf'
            - '.zip'
    condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate opening of documents/scripts from a mounted ISO or downloaded archive
    - IT helpdesk troubleshooting via .lnk shortcuts
level: high
ucs.rationale: Lazarus has spent five+ years on the same delivery shape — a zipped ISO/IMG containing a LNK that decodes a JS/VBS/PS stage-one (Mandiant 'IN-N-OUT', Microsoft Diamond Sleet 'TUNDRABRUSH'/'CLOUDBURST', ESET 'BlindingCan/MagicRAT', the Job-themed 3CX precursor, and the 2024 'Contagious Interview' npm-aside variant). The combination 'explorer.exe → script engine WHERE parent cmdline contains .iso/.l

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus BYOVD — vulnerable signed driver dropped/loaded/registered to disable EDR
ucs.description: Detects Lazarus Group's signature BYOVD (Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver) tradecraft used in operations such as Diamond Sleet's October-2023 attacks against EU/Asian defense and energy targets, the Lazarus 'YouAreAnIdiot' campaign, and the 3CX downstream intrusions. Triggers on file-create, image-load, or service-registration of the specific vulnerable signed drivers Lazarus repeatedly weaponises (Dell dbutil_2_3.sys, Avast aswArPot.sys, ENE ene.sys, MSI rtcore64.sys, Intel iqvw64e.sys, Gigabyte gdrv.sys, etc.) — the precursor to the FudModule/SHADOWPAD/RUSTBUCKET kernel-mode tampering that d
ucs.splunk:
(| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("dbutil_2_3.sys","aswArPot.sys","aswarpot.sys","ene.sys","enei.sys","wnbios.sys","wnbios64.sys","rwdrv.sys","iqvw64e.sys","rtcore64.sys","gdrv.sys","viragt64.sys","ucorewin.sys","tdsskiller.sys","ldrvprov.sys") AND Filesystem.action="created" by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | rename Filesystem.* AS * | eval signal="byovd_driver_dropped")
| append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) AS firstTime max(_time) AS lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Services\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*dbutil_2_3.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*aswArPot.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*aswarpot.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*rtcore64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*iqvw64e.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*ene.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*gdrv.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*wnbios64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*tdsskiller.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*viragt64.sys*") by host Registry.user Registry.process_name Registry.process Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data | rename Registry.* AS * | rename registry_value_data AS file_path | eval signal="byovd_service_registered", file_name="see file_path" ]
| where NOT match(process_name, "(?i)(msiexec|TrustedInstaller|setup)\.exe$")
| table firstTime host user signal process_name process file_name file_path
| sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let LazarusByovdDrivers = dynamic([
    "dbutil_2_3.sys","aswarpot.sys","ene.sys","enei.sys","wnbios.sys","wnbios64.sys",
    "rwdrv.sys","iqvw64e.sys","rtcore64.sys","gdrv.sys","viragt64.sys","ucorewin.sys",
    "tdsskiller.sys","ldrvprov.sys"
]);
let KnownInstallers = dynamic(["msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","setup.exe","dell.dellupdate.exe"]);
union isfuzzy=true
( DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName in~ (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (KnownInstallers)
    | extend Signal = "ByovdDriverDropped",
             Detail = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName),
             ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
             ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
             AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
             DriverSha = SHA256
    | project Timestamp, Signal, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentBinary, ParentCmd, Detail, DriverSha ),
( DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | extend Signal = "ByovdDriverLoaded",
             Detail = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName),
             ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
             ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
             AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
             DriverSha = SHA256
    | project Timestamp, Signal, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentBinary, ParentCmd, Detail, DriverSha ),
( DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | extend Signal = "ByovdServiceRegistered",
             Detail = RegistryValueData,
             ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
             ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
             AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
             DriverSha = ""
    | project Timestamp, Signal, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentBinary, ParentCmd, Detail, DriverSha )
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LazarusByovdDrivers = dynamic([
    "dbutil_2_3.sys","aswarpot.sys","ene.sys","enei.sys","wnbios.sys","wnbios64.sys",
    "rwdrv.sys","iqvw64e.sys","rtcore64.sys","gdrv.sys","viragt64.sys","ucorewin.sys",
    "tdsskiller.sys","ldrvprov.sys"
]);
union isfuzzy=true
( ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where tolower(TargetFileName) in (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | project TimeGenerated, Signal = "ByovdDriverDropped",
              DeviceName = DvcHostname, AccountName = ActorUsername,
              ParentBinary = ActingProcessName, ParentCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              Detail = TargetFilePath, DriverSha = TargetFileSHA256 ),
( WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 6
    | extend ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             DriverFile = tolower(extract(@"([^\\]+\.sys)$", 1, tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)))
    | where DriverFile in (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | project TimeGenerated, Signal = "ByovdDriverLoaded",
              DeviceName = Computer, AccountName = "",
              ParentBinary = "", ParentCmd = "",
              Detail = ImageLoaded,
              DriverSha = tostring(EventData.Hashes) ),
( ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Services\"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (LazarusByovdDrivers)
    | project TimeGenerated, Signal = "ByovdServiceRegistered",
              DeviceName = DvcHostname, AccountName = ActorUsername,
              ParentBinary = ActingProcessName, ParentCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              Detail = RegistryValueData, DriverSha = "" )
| where AccountName !endswith "$" or isempty(AccountName)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Lazarus Group is the threat actor most consistently observed weaponising vulnerable signed drivers as part of the FudModule rootkit / EDR-tampering tradecraft (AhnLab ASEC October 2022, ESET September 2023 'Operation DreamJob' DPRK-MagicLine campaign, Microsoft DEV-0139/Diamond Sleet 3CX downstream). The exact driver hash list overlaps repeatedly across Lazarus operations — dbutil_2_3.sys (Dell, C
